mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding employment income that drives consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising payroll months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.0% signals persistent labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns when UNRATE exceeds 8% are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales signal broad-based demand expansion that lifts aggregate corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint increases in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, month-over-month increases in consumer prices signal successful reflation that reduces real interest rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or CPIAUCSL increases during FEDFUNDS below 0.25% do not produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals manufacturing sector expansion that translates into higher aggregate corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or INDPRO growth months fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 650 thousand units signal weak residential investment that drags down construction employment and durable goods demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or HOUST below 650 coincides with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% combined with an unemployment rate above 8.0% signals a stimulative monetary environment without wage-driven inflation pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: high unemployment with zero interest rates
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint FEDFUNDS and UNRATE condition fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales signal weakening consumer spending that reduces near-term corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or RSAFS declines coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances.
